titles.en: Chapter on Judgment Regarding the Enclosure Between Two Houses
Chapter on Judgment Regarding the Enclosure Between Two Houses

segments: 3412 - سَأَلَ مَنْصُورُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ حَظِيرَةٍ بَيْنَ دَارَيْنِ فَذَكَرَ "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَضَى بِهَا لِصَاحِبِ اَلدَّارِ اَلَّذِي مِنْ قِبَلِهِ اَلْقِمَاطُ".
arabic.text: 3412 - سَأَلَ مَنْصُورُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ حَظِيرَةٍ بَيْنَ دَارَيْنِ فَذَكَرَ "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَضَى بِهَا لِصَاحِبِ اَلدَّارِ اَلَّذِي مِنْ قِبَلِهِ اَلْقِمَاطُ".

segments:
Hadith.3412 - Mansur ibn Hazim asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a courtyard situated between two houses.
Imam (as) mentioned that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled that it belongs to the owner of the house from whose side the boundary rope (al-qimat) extends.
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3412 - Mansur ibn Hazim asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a courtyard situated between two houses.
Imam (as) mentioned that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled that it belongs to the owner of the house from whose side the boundary rope (al-qimat) extends.

titles.en: Chapter on Judgment Regarding the Enclosure Between Two Houses

segments: رَجُلَيْنِ اِخْتَصَمَا إِلَيْهِ فِي خُصٍّ فَقَالَ "إِنَّ اَلْخُصَّ لِلَّذِي إِلَيْهِ اَلْقُمُطُ".
arabic.text: رَجُلَيْنِ اِخْتَصَمَا إِلَيْهِ فِي خُصٍّ فَقَالَ "إِنَّ اَلْخُصَّ لِلَّذِي إِلَيْهِ اَلْقُمُطُ".

segments:
Hadith.3413 - Amr ibn Shimr narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his father, from his grandfather, from Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as):
He ruled in a case where two men disputed over a reed fence (khuss).
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: "The fence belongs to the one to whom the boundary rope (al-qimat) is attached."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3413 - Amr ibn Shimr narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his father, from his grandfather, from Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as):
He ruled in a case where two men disputed over a reed fence (khuss).
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: "The fence belongs to the one to whom the boundary rope (al-qimat) is attached."

narratorChain.raw: 3413 - وَ رَوَى عَمْرُو بْنُ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ قَضَى فِي
